title: Edge session item (GET /edge/sessions)
description: One edge client's live session with its transport links — the Network surface's session table. Source of truth: doorman's session state (Phase 1 formalization per CONSOLE-CONTRACTS.md section 3.3; shapes per the Network spec, coordinated with PetalNet/doorman). A session survives link flaps by resuming; `links` are the underlying transports (primary/standby) with per-link health. state=floor means the session has fallen back to the last-resort transport. Filters: `handle`, `state`, plus the shared limit/cursor/since. Consumers MUST ignore unknown fields.

handle.description: Canonical handle of the enrolled edge client (edge-registry.handle).

host.description: Machine label in canonical form ('.N' for lab dot-hosts).

state.description: Session lifecycle: open = healthy; resuming = re-establishing after a link loss; floor = degraded to the last-resort transport; closed = ended.

established_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — when the session was first established.

resumes_count.description: How many times the session has resumed after a drop.

last_seen_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — most recent traffic/keepalive on the session.

handshakes_clean_count.description: Count of clean (fully verified) handshakes on this session; null when the daemon does not report it.

links.description: The transport links carrying this session.

link_id.description: Unique link id within the session.

role.description: primary carries traffic; standby is kept warm for failover.

state.description: Link health: active = carrying traffic; warm = ready standby; down = lost.

rtt_ms.description: Last measured round-trip time in ms; null when unmeasured/down.

established_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — when this link came up.

last_flap_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — most recent flap (down/up transition); null = never flapped.

flap_count_24h.description: Flaps in the trailing 24 hours (link stability signal).
